title: Trichelostylis tetragona
intro: Trichilia tetragona, also known as the "four-angled calabash tree", is a species of plant in the family Meliaceae. The health benefits of Trichilia tetragona include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce pain and inflammation, as well as protect against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of this plant include triterpenoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Trichilia tetragona include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues.
taxonomy: Trichelostylis tetragona is classified as a member of the family Menispermaceae, which is a family of flowering plants commonly known as moonseed family. This family consists of approximately 100 species of plants found in tropical and subtropical regions worldwide. Within the family, Trichelostylis tetragona belongs to the genus Trichelostylis, which is a group of flowering plants that are native to parts of Africa. The classification of Trichelostylis tetragona is based on its morphological characteristics, such as its leaves and flowers. Understanding the classification of a plant like Trichelostylis tetragona is crucial for its identification, conservation, and potential use in medicine and other industries.
